title: Pawn Structure
summary: Pawns are the soul of chess

steps.title: Philidor Was Right
steps.text: The 18th-century master Philidor said 'pawns are the soul of chess' — because pawn structure determines which pieces are active, which squares are weak, and where to play. Unlike pieces, pawns don't go backward. Every push is permanent.

steps.title: Pawn Weaknesses
steps.text: Learn to spot: isolated pawns (no pawn on adjacent files — hard to defend), doubled pawns (two on the same file — can't defend each other), backward pawns (stuck behind its neighbors — a permanent target), and passed pawns (unstoppable going forward).

steps.title: Structure Shapes the Plan
steps.text: The pawn structure tells you WHERE to attack. Caro-Kann players attack on the queenside, King's Indian players on the kingside — not by choice, but because that's where their pawns allow. Read the pawns, then make the plan.
